beats.activity: Wake up, coffee on the porch, checking weather and camera gear.
beats.reasoning: Grace starts Friday mornings with ritual coffee and observation; low energy yesterday (35/100) suggests she'll ease into the day. Clear, warm conditions match her habit of porch time.

beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library Capitol Hill Branch (Capitol Hill)
beats.reasoning: Standard commute from Redmond to Capitol Hill; she values the drive as thinking/listening time. 40 min budgeted for Friday traffic and across-town distance.

beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library Capitol Hill Branch (Capitol Hill)
beats.reasoning: Friday morning is typically busy at the library; Grace loves the grounding work of helping patrons and program planning. Four hours covers the morning shift before lunch break.

beats.location_name: Molly Moon's Homemade Ice Cream (Capitol Hill)
beats.reasoning: Grace gravitates to Molly Moon's on warm days for a light, refreshing meal (as yesterday). 45 min includes eating and photo-taking with her film camera in the nice weather.

beats.location_name: Seattle Public Library Capitol Hill Branch (Capitol Hill)
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift at the library, ending before 5pm Pride street fair closure kicks in (5pm Broadway/Pike closures). Grace can leave before traffic becomes chaotic.

beats.activity: Drive home to Redmond, avoiding Capitol Hill Pride street fair congestion.
beats.reasoning: Grace leaves before 5pm street closures; she values quiet over crowds. 40 min for evening commute across town, avoiding Pride congestion she's not drawn to.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and eating at home; reviewing film from the week.
beats.reasoning: Grace unwinds at home with a simple dinner and camera review, reflecting on the week's photos. Quiet evening suits her low energy baseline and preference for close time over big events.

beats.activity: Volunteer shift at the food bank: sorting, packing, organizing donations.
beats.reasoning: Friday evening volunteering is part of Grace's rhythm; Rainier Valley is a reasonable 20-min detour from home before settling in. Meaningful work with familiar volunteers energizes her despite low weekly energy.
Return commute from Rainier Valley to Redmond; 40 min evening drive. Grace is now home for the night.

beats.activity: Evening wind-down: porch time with a book, processing the week, preparing for bed.
beats.reasoning: Grace ends the day as she began it—on the porch, this time with a book and reflection. The warm, breezy evening is perfect for her to decompress before sleep.
Grace sleeps 6 hours 45 minutes, ending before 06:00 on Saturday. The beat spans overnight as required.

diary: Friday morning started quiet on the porch with coffee—the weather was perfect, one of those breezy June days that makes you remember why you live here. Drove to Capitol Hill listening to NPR, already thinking about the Pride festivities that would take over the neighborhood later. The library was steady all morning; we're ramping up the summer reading program and I found myself genuinely excited talking to families about it. Grabbed lunch at Molly Moon's and took some film photos of the street—the light was just right. Afternoon flew by at the reference desk. I made sure to leave early to avoid the Pride street fair chaos, though part of me wanted to stay and soak it in. Made dinner at home, then headed to my volunteer shift at the food bank in Rainier Valley. There's something grounding about sorting donations, organizing things into neat boxes. Drove home as the sun was setting, and spent the evening on the porch with a book, just breathing. Felt the week settle into my bones.
thought_process: I deliberately chose to leave Capitol Hill before the evening festivities—not out of discomfort, but practicality. The drive home would've been nightmarish, and honestly, I needed the quiet. My mood stayed steady today, buoyed by the good weather and purposeful work. The library work energized me in its way, and the food bank volunteer shift grounded me when I might've otherwise felt scattered. Photography at lunch was a small rebellion—a moment just for me. I think I'm at a point in my life where I value intention over spectacle. Pride will happen whether I'm in the crowd or not; what matters is that I showed up for the people I serve, organized something real with my hands, and ended the day at peace.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
